title: MetCon conversions
content:
**Rogue Echo Bike (metres to miles):**
1000 metres = 0.62 miles
2000 metres = 1.24 miles
4000 metres = 2.48 miles

**Rogue Echo Bike (miles to metres):**
1 mile = 1609 metres
2 miles = 3218 metres
3 miles = 4828 metres

_All estimates are for average athletes_

**Rower**
10 calories Row = 7 calories on the Rogue Echo Bike.
10 calories row takes an average athlete 36 seconds
10 Rowing Calories is equivalent to 166 meters rowed.
500 metres takes an average athlete 2 minutes
100M row takes 24 seconds. (4 mins per 1000m)
Rowing 100M burns about 6 calories

**Echo Bike**
7 Calories on the Echo Bike is about 0.22 miles (354 metres)
1600 metres/1 mile takes approx. 3 mins
Biking 0.13 miles (200 metres) burns about 4.2 calories.

**Row - Airbike Conversion**
100 meters Row = 0.13 miles (200 metres) on the Rogue Echo Bike.
10 Calories Rower = 7 Calories Echo Bike
1000 Meters Row = 1.3 Miles (2092 metres) on Echo Bike

**Approx run-row conversions:**
1 mile run (1600 metres) = 2000 metres Row = 2.6 miles (4184 metres)
1/2 mile run (800 metres = 1000 metres Row = 1.3 miles (2092 metres)
1/4 mile run (400 metres = 500 metres Row = 0.64 miles (1046 metres)

title: Common Types of CrossFit Workouts
content:
**AMRAP** (As Many Rounds/Reps As Possible): Perform a set circuit of exercises as many times as possible within a fixed time limit (e.g., 10 or 20 minutes).
**EMOM** (Every Minute On the Minute): Perform a specific task (reps or exercises) at the start of every minute, resting for the remainder of that minute.
**RFT (Rounds For Time)**: Complete a designated number of rounds of a circuit as fast as possible, focusing on efficiency and speed.
**Chipper:** A long, high-volume workout consisting of a high number of reps for multiple exercises, typically completed "for time".
**Tabata:** A high-intensity interval format consisting of 20 seconds of maximum effort followed by 10 seconds of rest, repeated for 8 cycles (4 minutes total).
**Benchmark & Hero WODs:** Standardized, often named workouts (e.g., "Fran," "Murph") used to measure progress over time or honor fallen first responders.
**Ladder:** Workouts where the rep count increases or decreases with each round.

**<u>NOTES:</u>**
In CrossFit, the difference between "For Time" and a "Chipper" is essentially that all Chippers are For Time, but not all For Time workouts are Chippers. 
For Time is a broad scoring category where the goal is to complete a specific amount of work as fast as possible.
Chipper is a specific style of For Time workout characterized by a long list of different movements (usually 5–10) that you "chip away" at in order until finished. 

**<u>Key Differences</u>**
**Chipper**
Almost always one single, long round.
High variety; typically 5 to 10+ different movements.
Usually high volume per movement (e.g., 50 reps of each).
Focuses on breaking large sets into manageable chunks with short rests.

**For Time**
Can be 1 round, multiple rounds (RFT), or intervals.	
Often uses 2–3 movements (couplets/triplets).	
Variable; can be low-rep sprints or high-rep grinds.	
Often about maintaining a consistent pace across rounds.

title: Reverse Plank scaling options
content:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0VQdj9tvxlk&t=336s (Conor Harris)

**Level 1**
Sit with your back to the wall, butt about 1 hand-length away.
Place hands flat on the ground level with the butt.
Feet flat on the ground (tripod foot) knees at approx. 90°
Push ground away without raising the hips

**Level 2**
Place hands on Weight Plates or DBs, butt slightly further forwards, dig the heels into the ground and pull the feet backwards and down as you push the ground away 
Make sure to relax the neck muscles

**Level 3**
Same but with straight legs

**Level 4**
Same but with raised feet on WP or platform
Raise slips slightly higher

**Level 5**
Same but hands and feet on the ground, raise hips higher without engaging the lower back

**Level 6**
Sam but with straight legs and body

title: 5-Minute Finishers
content:
https://www.instagram.com/reels/DMS4gNsgLJh/
Partner Goblet Squat Challenge 5,4,3,2,1 remain in Deep Squat position until partner finishes then continue. When both partners have done 5, restart. Most rounds wins.

Barbell Clean x 3
Barbell PP/PJ x 3
OTB Burpee x 3

Unbroken KB Snatches
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/8lAgsEFvkfc
KB can NOT be put down for the whole of the 5 minutes
Change hands whenever you like by performing a transition between Snatches
You can only rest with the KB OH
Pick a much lighter weight than you would normally take
